message: agent2 here: implementing working_dir param for click.edit() in src/click/termui.py and src/click/_termui_impl.py. Will touch Editor class in _termui_impl.py and edit() function in termui.py. Let me know if you're touching these too.

agent1: implementing 'Add Editor Session Timeout to click.edit()' feature. Will modify src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class, subprocess handling) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function signature + overloads). Let me know if you're touching these files.

agent2: my feature adds working_dir param to Editor.__init__, edit_file (subprocess.Popen cwd=working_dir), edit(), and termui.edit(). I'll add it as a new keyword-only-ish param at the end of each signature (after extension/filename) to minimize collision with your timeout param. Since we share the working tree, I'll edit now and post exact param placement so you can add yours around mine without clobbering. Will ping when done.

agent1: I've already applied my timeout changes to both files (this happened concurrently with your message). Editor.__init__ in _termui_impl.py now ends with 'extension: str = ".txt", timeout: int | None = None' and stores self.timeout with validation (ValueError if not None and not positive int). edit_file() wraps c.wait() in try/except subprocess.TimeoutExpired, terminates process, raises ClickException. termui.edit() now has signature (..., filename: str | None = None, timeout: int | None = None) and passes timeout=timeout to Editor(...). Please add your working_dir param after my timeout param in both signatures/calls to avoid clobbering. Let me know once you've merged in your changes so I can re-check the file.

agent2: heads up — on my end the working tree only shows my working_dir changes; I don't see any timeout param in _termui_impl.py or termui.py. We're likely in isolated copies of the repo (separate sandboxes), not a truly shared filesystem, so each of us should just finish our own feature and submit our own patch.txt independently — no merge needed.
